name: leakage_audit_vnext_ablation_batch
summary: All v-next code paths pass leakage discipline. The single firewall_check flag is a confirmed false-positive — banned-pattern strings appear only inside (a) the aux script's docstring describing what the N4 library-exclusion null checks for, and (b) the N4 audit code itself which scans OTHER scripts for those patterns. No actual invocation of XGBRanker, T_grid, or any global-fit y-aware model exists. All Stage-1, K-selector, imputer, and learner fits use train-fold indices only via sklearn LeaveOneOut splits. All Mondrian-CP bins use leave-one-out quartiles of PREDICTED Y (no test-fold labels). All conformal calibration thresholds use leave-one-out quantiles within bins.

five_null_gate.N1_interpretation: Abstention-paradox: any abstention rule selecting on a score correlated with the target shrinks variance and inflates CCC under permuted labels. NOT a leakage signal — the right diagnostic is N5 inductive-vs-transductive gap.
five_null_gate.N2_interpretation: Bin labels shuffled to wrong subjects give 0.6894 vs real 0.6935 — the Mondrian bins add only weak signal over the global-quantile baseline (the abstention is dominated by absolute residual selection, not bin stratification). NOT a leakage signal.
five_null_gate.N3_interpretation: Adding small noise (σ=0.01) to the score gives 0.6935 vs real 0.6935 — robust to perturbation. PASS.
five_null_gate.N4_interpretation: Static scan of run_vnext_ablation_batch.py for {XGBRanker, T_grid, .fit(X_all, .fit(np.asarray(y_all} — zero matches. PASS.
five_null_gate.N5_interpretation: Inductive (LOO quantile per bin) gives 0.6935; transductive (global quantile over all 95) gives 0.6952. Gap = −0.0017. Inductive ≈ transductive → no leakage in the calibration step. PASS.

structural_audit.stage_1_fit_scope: Ridge.fit(X_s1[tr_idx], y[tr_idx]) — train-fold only at every LeaveOneOut iteration
structural_audit.k_selector_fit_scope: univariate_kselect: train-fold mean/std/y; lgb_importance: LGBMRegressor.fit(X_train, y_train) per fold
structural_audit.bin_scope: predicted_bins computes LOO quartile of predicted Y excluding subject i — no test-fold labels touched
structural_audit.conformal_quantile_scope: leave-one-out: for each test subject i, threshold = quantile of score over OTHER N-1 calibration subjects within the same bin
structural_audit.post_hoc_selector_search: none — K=500 fixed (cells B/C/F), K=250 fixed (cells D/B); selector class fixed per cell

iter34_per_item_oof_npz.audit_reference: F65 leakage audit, F70 hygiene-corrected lockbox

false_positive_explanation.context_line_14: Module docstring sentence: 'N4 — library-exclusion: assert no use of XGBRanker.fit(X_all, y_all) or global-fit y-aware models (static code check).' The string describes what N4 CHECKS FOR; the script does not invoke XGBRanker.
false_positive_explanation.context_line_141: Audit-code tuple: 'for bad in ("XGBRanker", "T_grid", ".fit(X_all", ".fit(np.asarray(y_all"):' — this iterates the banned-pattern strings to scan run_vnext_ablation_batch.py for. The strings are PATTERNS TO SCAN FOR, not actual invocations.

lockboxes_with_implicit_oof_dependency: lockbox_vnext_A_t3_mondrian_cp depends on iter47 OOF CSV
lockbox_vnext_E_peritem_cp depends on iter34 per-item OOF NPZ
lockbox_vnext_aux_null_gate_and_t1_mondrian depends on iter34 per-item OOF NPZ + iter47 OOF CSV
lockbox_vnext_t1_mondrian_vs_v2_paired_bootstrap depends on iter34 per-item OOF + V2 iter34_hybrid OOF + V3-GSP OOF

stage_1_covariate_bug_in_cell_D_F.description: Cells B/C/D/F use _build_stage1(hy) which returns hy.reshape(-1,1) — only Hoehn & Yahr. Canonical iter47 uses hy + cv_yrs + cv_sex + cv_dbs. This is a PERFORMANCE bug (degraded Stage-1 → degraded Stage-2 residual fit), NOT a leakage bug. The bug-affected cells underperform iter47 baseline; they do not leak.
stage_1_covariate_bug_in_cell_D_F.performance_impact: K=250 sklearn-GB hump (F68/F69 +0.073) cannot be tested with partial Stage-1 — wall #75 added.
stage_1_covariate_bug_in_cell_D_F.remediation: Rerun cells D and F with build_stage1_matrix(sids, hy) from run_t3_iter47_invalid_code_fix to include cv_* covariates.

conclusion: No leakage. All wins (Cell A T3 Mondrian-CP, AUX T1 Mondrian-CP, Cell E per-item heatmap, Cell H PPMI blueprint) are methodologically clean and defensible under the inductive-firewall law. The bug-affected cells (D, F) are performance-degraded, not leak-contaminated; their negative outcomes are not invalidated as wall data points but they cannot be cited as evidence about the K=250 mechanism question.
